methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $250M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Twelve Labs, a Series A AI & Software company founded by Jae Lee and Aiden Lee, is valued at $250M post-money after raising $57M to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers, including 8 private companies (Liquid AI, Field AI, xAI, and others) and 1 public comparable (Palantir). The peer set ranges from $500M to $32.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier to the $250M last post-money, implying a valuation of $500M. Peer-Median analysis across the 9-company comp set yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range spanning $500M (floor) to $32.0B (ceiling). The peer median is materially elevated by later-stage outliers such as Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B, Series B) and Physical Intelligence ($5.6B, Series B), while same-stage comparable Liquid AI is valued at $2.4B.

The two methods produce a wide spread. The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.4B. Key uncertainties include (1) the quality and comparability of the peer set, given the extreme range and heterogeneous stage distribution; (2) Twelve Labs' progress on product, revenue, or other unit economics relative to peers; and (3) whether the current $250M reflects true Series A pricing or an interim valuation. The median estimate suggests meaningful upside from current valuation, though downside risk exists if peer comps compress.
